title: SELECT DISTINCT not removing duplicates
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table(id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), value FLOAT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'apple', 1.1), (2, 'banana', 2.2), (3, 'apple', 1.1), (4, 'banana', 2.3);
SELECT DISTINCT name, value FROM test_table;
SELECT DISTINCT CASE WHEN value > 2.0 THEN name ELSE NULL END AS filtered_name FROM test_table;
SELECT name, COUNT(*) AS name_count FROM test_table GROUP BY name HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
key_bug_features: SELECT DISTINCT not functioning as expected with duplicates
